question: Has the root cause been addressed, not just symptoms?
summary: Root cause (markMessagesRead only fires on selection change, not on message arrival) is directly addressed by adding useEffect that watches unreadCounts and calls markMessagesRead when messages arrive in open conversation. Bug report lines 42-54 describe the sequence, contract lines 6-14 identify exact root cause in onSelectContact callback not firing on message arrival. Implementation at main.tsx:2197-2206 adds the monitoring needed. Tests at bug-unread-badge-not-clearing.test.ts:83-141 verify the fix works. No symptoms-only fixes detected.

question: Does the implementation contain changes not documented in the fix contract?
summary: Only src/renderer/main.tsx was modified (15 lines added: useEffect hook). .claude/verification-results.csv changed due to logging, not implementation. Fix contract specifies exactly one change (useEffect in main.tsx after line 2191) which matches implementation. No undocumented changes in production code detected. Regression test added in separate file (bug-unread-badge-not-clearing.test.ts) as expected collateral in contract.

question: Does a regression test exist that would catch this bug if reintroduced?
summary: Yes. A comprehensive regression test exists at src/renderer/nostling/bug-unread-badge-not-clearing.test.ts with 4 test cases, including the specific test 'REGRESSION TEST: After fix, auto-mark messages read when they arrive in open conversation' that validates the bug fix.

question: Are there any parallel implementations?
summary: No parallel implementations found. The fix adds a useEffect hook (main.tsx:2199-2206) that calls nostling.markMessagesRead when unreadCounts changes. The existing onSelectContact callback (main.tsx:2438) also calls the same function. Both call the identical markMessagesRead from state.ts:474, which calls the same backend service (service.ts:561). This is complementary not parallel—onSelectContact handles manual selection, useEffect handles automatic marking when messages arrive in open conversation. Different triggers, same implementation, no duplication.

question: Are there similar patterns in the codebase that might have the same bug?
summary: No similar patterns detected. The unread badge clearing bug is isolated to the interaction between polling (main.tsx:2188 refreshUnreadCounts every 3s) and the onSelectContact callback (main.tsx:2434-2440). The polling updates unreadCounts state but was not monitored to trigger the marking handler. Contract explicitly confirms 'this is the only location where unread badge clearing is handled' (line 60). Searched entire renderer for similar polling+handler patterns: conversation poller (utils/conversation-poller.ts) only refreshes messages, no handler needed; p2p status poller (main.tsx:1918-1934) just updates status display; theme polling absent. No code duplication or similar missing handlers found.

question: Does evictToQuota enforce the storage quota (default 500MB) via LRU eviction of the oldest uploaded blobs, and does it never evict unuploaded (queued) blobs?

question: Does evictToQuota enforce the storage quota (default 500MB) via LRU eviction of the oldest uploaded blobs, and does it never evict unuploaded (queued) blobs?
summary: evictToQuota correctly enforces 500MB storage quota with LRU eviction of oldest uploaded blobs (ordered by uploaded_at ASC). Never evicts unuploaded blobs. All test scenarios pass including edge cases.

question: Is there any duplicated logic between deleteExpiredBlobs and evictToQuota that should be consolidated?
summary: Both methods contain an identical 6-line WHERE clause filtering for uploaded blobs with all message_media references in 'uploaded' status. However, the methods differ significantly in filtering conditions (time-based vs. no time filter), SELECT columns, and iteration logic, making full consolidation impractical. The duplication is minor and acceptable given the distinct purposes of the two methods.
